content.text:
I'll start by reading the essential harness files to understand the current state of the project.

Let me read the progress file first.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Read">
<parameter name="file_path">/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/claude-progress.txt</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<result>
<function_result>
# Claude Progress Log

## Session History

### Session 1 - Initial Setup
- Reviewed project structure and requirements
- Created comprehensive documentation in CLAUDE.md
- Established harness protocol with features.json
- Ready for feature implementation

### Session 2 - Monorepo Debugging
- Fixed monorepo configuration issues
- Vite was not running properly
- Identified configuration problems
- Created troubleshooting documentation

### Session 3 - Astro to Vite Migration Research
- Analyzed why Astro was causing complexity
- Recommended migration to pure Vite + React
- Updated architecture documents

### Session 4 - Vite Migration Execution
- Created new Vite + React app structure
- Encountered BlueprintJS 6 compatibility issues
- React 18 vs React 19 conflicts
- Session ended mid-migration

### Session 5 - BlueprintJS + React 18 Fix
- Diagnosed peer dependency conflicts
- Downgraded to React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)
- Fixed @react-three/fiber compatibility (v8 for React 18)
- All dependencies now compatible

### Session 6 - R3F Canvas Implementation
- Implemented InfiniteCanvas.tsx with R3F
- Added placeholder layers (Macro, Meso, Micro)
- Created MesoLayer with InstancedMesh (blocks render)
- Added CameraController with zoom/pan
- Visual test: Canvas renders blue blocks, zoom/pan works
- **Status:** F103, F105, F106 substantially complete

### Session 7 - Visual Testing & Feature Completion (2024-12-16 ~02:00)
- Conducted comprehensive visual testing via Playwright MCP
- Verified MesoLayer renders colored blocks correctly
- Verified pan (drag) and zoom (scroll) work
- Tested LOD transitions visually
- Fixed issues found during testing
- Marked F103, F105, F106, F107 as complete
- **Status:** Core foundation complete, ready for F104

### Session 8 - Blueprint Integration & App Improvements (2024-12-16)
- Added BlueprintJS Navbar with zoom indicator
- Added LOD indicator Tag in navbar
- Added reset view button
- Improved overall app layout structure
- **Visual verification:** All UI components render correctly
- Integrated BlueprintJS styling properly with R3F canvas
- Ready for next features (F108+)

### Session 9 - MacroLayer Shader Implementation (2024-12-16)
- Implemented MacroLayer with ShaderMaterial
- Created DataTexture for heatmap encoding
- Integrated with LOD system (zoom < 0.5)
- Yellow → Orange → Red gradient based on occupancy
- Visual test: Heatmap renders correctly at low zoom
- **Status:** F108 complete

### Session 10 - MicroLayer HTML Implementation (2024-12-16)
- Implemented MicroLayer with drei Html component
- Task cards appear at zoom > 2.0
- Cards show task type, duration, dates, train info
- Conflict tasks have red styling
- Smooth LOD transition from Meso to Micro
- **Status:** F109 complete

### Session 11 - Sidebar Implementation (2024-12-16)
- Created TrainSidebar component with BlueprintJS Card
- Virtual scroll sync with canvas panY
- Train IDs display correctly
- Fixed layout issues with sidebar positioning
- **Status:** F111 complete

### Session 12 - Zoom Controls Implementation (2024-12-16)
- Created ZoomControls component with BlueprintJS ButtonGroup
- +/- buttons for zoom control
- Positioned bottom-right of canvas
- Reset view integrated into zoom controls
- Visual verification: Controls work correctly
- **Status:** F112 complete

### Session 13 - Timeline Header Implementation (2024-12-16)
- Created TimelineHeader component
- Adaptive date labels based on zoom level
- Synced with camera X position (panX)
- Year/Month/Day granularity transitions
- Fixed positioning above canvas
- **Status:** F113 complete

### Session 14 - LOD Indicator Enhancement (2024-12-16)
- Already implemented in Session 8 as part of navbar
- Tag component shows MACRO/MESO/MICRO with Intent colors
- F114 was essentially complete, just needed verification
- **Status:** F114 verified complete

### Session 15 - Instructions Toast Implementation (2024-12-16)
- Created InstructionsToast component
- Auto-hiding onboarding message using BlueprintJS Toaster
- Shows pan/zoom instructions on first load
- Dismisses after 5 seconds or on click
- **Status:** F115 complete

### Session 16 - Conflict Indicators Implementation (2024-12-16)
- Conflicts already visible in MesoLayer (red color)
- Added conflict icon/warning to MicroLayer cards
- Enhanced conflict visibility in task cards
- Visual verification: Conflicts clearly marked
- **Status:** F110 complete

### Session 17 - Performance Verification (2024-12-16)
- Verified 60fps rendering via console performance logs
- Tested with 50 trains × 10 years data
- Zoom/pan maintains smooth frame rate
- LOD transitions are instant
- No memory leaks detected in extended use
- **Status:** F116 verified complete

### Session 18 - Visual Parity Check (2024-12-16)
- Compared production app with POC reference
- All LOD levels match expected appearance
- Sidebar, timeline, controls all present
- Minor visual polish applied
- **Status:** F117 complete

### Session 19 - State Management Migration (2024-12-16)
- Researched state management approaches for WebGL
- Created comprehensive research document (docs/state-management-research/)
- Documented dual-store architecture plan
- Added STATE-ARCH-001 to features.json
- **Status:** Research phase complete

---

## ⚠️ KNOWN ISSUES / TECH DEBT

### TECH-DEBT-001: State Management Modernization [COMPLETED]
**Resolved in Session 20-21**
- Successfully migrated from React hooks to @mms/toolkit state-machine
- Single canonical machine in apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts
- All components use useCanvasSelector and canvasActions
- No more stale closures in animation loops

The following components use the new state-machine:
- CameraController.tsx - viewport state and camera updates
- InfiniteCanvas.tsx - LOD state, hover state
- TrainSidebar.tsx - panY subscription
- TimelineHeader.tsx - panX and zoom subscription
- MicroLayer.tsx - selection state

### TECH-DEBT-002: Organize Layer Components [TODO]
The layers folder structure could be improved:
- MacroLayer.tsx - 200+ lines with inline shader
- Consider extracting shaders to separate .glsl files
- Consider extracting color utilities

### TECH-DEBT-003: Mock Data Generator Enhancement [TODO]
Current generator is basic:
- Could add more realistic maintenance patterns
- Could add proper dependencies between tasks
- Could add resource constraint simulation

---

## Current Status

### Completed Features (F101-F117)
All production app features are complete:
- ✅ F101: Project Setup (Vite + React 18)
- ✅ F102: BlueprintJS 6 Integration
- ✅ F103: R3F Canvas Setup
- ✅ F104: Data Types & Generator
- ✅ F105: MesoLayer (InstancedMesh)
- ✅ F106: CameraController (zoom/pan)
- ✅ F107: LOD State Management
- ✅ F108: MacroLayer (shader heatmap)
- ✅ F109: MicroLayer (HTML cards)
- ✅ F110: Conflict Indicators
- ✅ F111: TrainSidebar
- ✅ F112: ZoomControls
- ✅ F113: TimelineHeader
- ✅ F114: LOD Indicator
- ✅ F115: Instructions Toast
- ✅ F116: Performance Optimization
- ✅ F117: Visual Parity

### In Progress
- STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Architecture (Phase 1-4)

### Ready for Next Session
The production demo is complete and working.

Next priorities:
1. STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 2: Implement entity store with TypedArrays
2. Add more interactive features (F02x series)
3. Implement accessibility features (F021-F022)

---

## Session 20 - State Machine Setup (2024-12-16)
- Analyzed @mms/toolkit package structure
- Created canvas-machine.ts with proper slices
- Set up viewport slice for camera, zoom, momentum
- Set up interaction slice for hover, selection
- Ready for component integration
- **Status:** STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 1a complete

### Key Files Modified
- packages/toolkit/src/core/create-slice.ts - Fixed types
- apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts - New machine
- apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts - Viewport slice
- apps/canvas/src/state/interaction.ts - Interaction slice
- apps/canvas/src/state/index.ts - Re-exports

---

## Session 21 - Component Migration to State Machine (2024-12-16)
- Migrated CameraController to use canvasStore
- Migrated InfiniteCanvas to use useCanvasSelector
- Migrated TrainSidebar to subscribe to panY
- Migrated TimelineHeader to use viewport state
- Migrated MicroLayer to use selection state
- Fixed stale closure issues in animation loops
- **Status:** TECH-DEBT-001 COMPLETE

### Key Changes
- Removed useState/useRef for state, using canvasStore.getState()
- Components use useCanvasSelector for reactive updates
- Animation loops use direct getState() for synchronous access
- All state flows through single canvas-machine

### Verification
- Visual test: All components render correctly
- Pan/zoom still works smoothly
- LOD transitions work
- Sidebar syncs with canvas
- Timeline syncs with camera position

---

## Session 22 - TECH-DEBT-001 Final Cleanup (2024-12-16)
- Cleaned up dead code and unused imports
- Verified all components use state machine consistently
- Updated type exports in canvas-machine.ts
- **Status:** TECH-DEBT-001 fully complete

### Architecture Summary
```
apps/canvas/src/state/
├── canvas-machine.ts   # Main machine with createStore
├── viewport.ts         # Camera, zoom, momentum, animation
├── interaction.ts      # Hover, selection, mouse position
└── index.ts            # Re-exports: canvasStore, canvasActions, useCanvasSelector
```

### Usage Pattern
```typescript
// Read (reactive - causes re-render)
const zoom = useCanvasSelector(s => s.viewport.camera.zoom);

// Read (synchronous - for animation loops)
const camera = canvasStore.getState().viewport.camera;

// Write
canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setCamera({ x, y, zoom }));
```

---

## Session 23 - D3 Migration Phase 1 (2024-12-17)
- Installed d3-array, d3-scale, d3-time, d3-time-format
- Created d3 utility modules in apps/canvas/src/utils/d3/
- Implemented createTimeScale, createLinearScale for coordinate mapping
- Implemented formatDate, formatDateRange for timeline labels
- Implemented extent, bisect for data analysis
- All utilities are tree-shakeable (named exports)
- **Status:** D3-MIGRATE-001 Phase 1 complete

### Key Files Created
- apps/canvas/src/utils/d3/index.ts - Re-exports
- apps/canvas/src/utils/d3/scales.ts - d3-scale + d3-time wrappers
- apps/canvas/src/utils/d3/time-format.ts - d3-time-format wrappers
- apps/canvas/src/utils/d3/array.ts - d3-array utilities

### Notes for Future Sessions
- Phase 2: Replace timeline formatting with d3 utilities
- Phase 3: Use d3-scale-chromatic for MacroLayer heatmap colors
- Phase 4: Evaluate d3-shape for potential future visualizations

---

## Session 24 - D3 Migration Phase 2 (2024-12-17)
- Migrated TimelineHeader to use d3 time formatting
- Replaced manual date formatting with formatTimelineLabel utility
- Using d3-time-format for locale-aware formatting
- Improved granularity selection with d3 time intervals
- **Status:** D3-MIGRATE-001 Phase 2 complete

### Key Changes
- TimelineHeader.tsx now uses formatTimelineLabel from d3 utils
- Added formatTimelineLabel function for zoom-adaptive formatting
- Granularity: days (high zoom) → weeks → months → years (low zoom)

---

## Session 25 - D3 Migration Phase 3 (2024-12-17)
- Installed d3-scale-chromatic and d3-interpolate
- Created color scale utilities using d3 sequential scales
- Updated MacroLayer shader to use d3-generated color ramp
- Replaced hardcoded yellow→orange→red with YlOrRd color scheme
- **Status:** D3-MIGRATE-001 Phase 3 complete

### Key Files Modified
- apps/canvas/src/utils/d3/colors.ts - NEW: d3-scale-chromatic wrappers
- apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx - Uses d3 color ramp
- apps/canvas/src/utils/d3/index.ts - Re-exports color utilities

### Technical Notes
- generateColorRamp() creates 256-step gradient for shader
- Using interpolateYlOrRd from d3-scale-chromatic
- Color ramp passed to shader as uniform array
- Shader interpolates between ramp colors based on occupancy

---

## Session 26 - D3 Migration Documentation (2024-12-17)
- Created comprehensive migration documentation
- Documented all phases with code examples
- Added architecture diagrams
- Provided guidelines for future d3 usage
- **Status:** D3-MIGRATE-001 FULLY COMPLETE

### Documentation Created
- docs/d3-migration/README.md - Complete migration guide
- Covers: scales, time formatting, colors, array utilities
- Includes: usage examples, architecture, future roadmap

---

## Session 27 - Deck Research (2024-12-17)
- Created deck integration research document
- Analyzed Deck.gl architecture (deck-core, standalone layers)
- Documented hybrid architecture possibility (R3F + Deck layers)
- Identified use cases: minimap, annotations, statistics overlays
- **Status:** Research complete, documented in docs/deck-research/

### Key Findings
- Deck.gl can render to external WebGL context
- Possible to overlay Deck layers on R3F canvas
- Best for 2D overlays and data visualizations
- Current R3F implementation sufficient for core features
- Deck integration is OPTIONAL enhancement

---

## Next Session Priorities

1. **STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 2**: Entity Store with TypedArrays
   - Implement Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern
   - Create TaskEntityStore with TypedArrays
   - Add spatial indexing with RBush

2. **Interactive Features** (F02x series):
   - F023: Dependency lines between tasks
   - F025: Minimap navigation
   - F026: Task details panel

3. **Accessibility** (F021-F022):
   - F021: Keyboard navigation
   - F022: ARIA labels

---

## Environment Notes

- **Runtime:** Bun 1.2.0
- **Node:** v22.x (via Bun)
- **OS:** macOS Darwin
- **Working Dir:** /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system

## Quick Commands

```bash
# Run production app
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# Type check
bun run typecheck

# Visual test (via Playwright MCP)
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
```
</function_result>
</result>

I have read the progress file. The project is a Metro Integrated Planning System with a semantic zoom canvas visualization. The core features (F101-F117) are complete, and recent work has focused on state management migration and D3 integration.

I'm ready to assist with planning tasks. What would you like me to help you design or explore in the codebase?
